subject: Reasoning & Mental Ability
note: Practice feed me correct answer nahi hota; jaha ID important-questions se match kiya waha answer bhara hai (answer_source dekho).

questions.question_text: Mountain : Valley :: Genius : ?
questions.solution: The word in each pair are antonyms of each other.

questions.question_text: Identify the one which is different from others: 324, 244, 136, 352, 514
questions.solution: Except for 324, the sum of each digit in each number is 10.

The relationship is x : x

(x + 1) = 7(7 + 1) = 56

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 56

9(9 + 1) = 90

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 90

questions.question_text: Identify the odd letter group? XW, FG, ML, PO, TS
questions.solution: Except all other groups contains two consecutive letters in reverse order.

questions.question_text:
Select the one which is different from the other three responses.
1, 65, 8, 64
Select the one which is different from other three responses.
questions.solution: In letter group AIUE, all letters are Vowels.

questions.question_text: In the following question, select the odd one from the given alternatives.
questions.solution: Expect disease, all other terms denote obstruction, hindrance or interruption.

questions.question_text:
In the following question, select the odd word from the given alternatives.
36-72, 17-34, 28-49, 24-48
questions.solution: Except 28-49, all other pairs are like, the second number is double the first number.

questions.question_text: In the following question, select odd word from the given alternatives.
questions.solution: Expect Polo, all others are indoor games.

questions.question_text:
Find the odd one out:
Sketch, Paper, Poster, Diagram
questions.solution: All others are drawn on paper.

questions.question_text: Which word does not belong with the others?
questions.solution: The wing, fin, and rudder are all parts of an airplane.

questions.question_text: Find odd one out:
questions.solution: All except Telescope are audio devices.

questions.question_text:
Find the odd one out:
Polyester, Cotton, Terylene, Nylon
questions.solution: All except Cotton are synthetic fibres while cotton is a natural fibre.

questions.question_text: Find the odd one out: Geometry, Algebra, Trigonometry, Mathematics
questions.solution: All except Mathematics are branches of Mathematics.

questions.question_text: In question find the odd letters from the given alternatives.
questions.solution:
All the other options are in alphabetical order, so this option should have read ‘PQRS‘, but instead reads ‘PRSQ’, and is therefore incorrect.

Hence, ‘PQSR’ is odd one out.

questions.question_text:
Choose the number which is different from the rest:
21, 69, 81, 83
questions.solution: 81 is the only perfect square number in the group.

questions.question_text:
Which pair does not belong with the others?
62-37, 74-40, 47-22, 103-78
questions.solution:
In all other pairs, the difference between the two numbers is 25.

For 74-40 difference is 34.

questions.question_text:
Select the one which is different from the other three responses.
82 - 73, 66-58, 26-17, 55-46
questions.solution:
Except pair (66 – 58), all other pairs’ difference is 9.

82 – 73 = 9

26 – 17 = 9

55 – 46 = 9

But

66 – 58 = 8

questions.question_text: Which of the following options is different from the others?
questions.solution:
There is a gap of 1 alphabet in between PR.

There is a gap of 2 alphabets in between AD.

There is a gap of 2 alphabets in between LO.

There is a gap of 2 alphabets in between EH.

So, PR is different from the others.

questions.question_text: Find the number that will replace the question mark (?). 6 is to 42 as 8 is to ___?___.
questions.solution:
As, 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 42

Similarly, 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 56

questions.question_text: Find the term which is different from the others.
questions.solution: All except honey are produced by plants or trees. But honey is produced by bees.

questions.question_text: Identify the relationship of the first pair and then find the missing term in the second pair. 1, 2, 3 : 2, 3, 4 :: 3, 4, 5 : ?
Cube is related to the square in the same way as the square is related to ________.
questions.solution: The cube comprises squares on all of its surfaces. In the same way, the square comprises lines on all of its sides.

questions.question_text:
Select the one which is different from the other three responses.
23, 25, 19, 17
questions.solution:
23, 19, and 17 are prime numbers whereas 25 is not.

Therefore 25 is the odd one out.

questions.question_text: In each of the following questions, select the one which is different from the other three reasons.
questions.solution: All other options are upper wear except Shorts which is bottom wear.

questions.question_text:
In the following question, select the odd word from the given alternatives.
Cricket, Volley Ball, Chess, Hockey
questions.solution: All are outdoor games except chess.

questions.question_text: In the following options, ________ is different from the others.
questions.solution: Roar-Bull is the only wrong pair of animal and sound of that particular animal. The bull does not roar. Hence, the pair "Roar- Bull" is wrong.

questions.question_text: Select the odd word/letters/number/number pair from the given alternatives.
questions.solution: Expect 14-48, all other second number is completely divisible by the first number.

questions.question_text:
Choose the odd one out.
421, 835, 725, 945
questions.solution:
As, 8 - 3 = 5, 7 - 2 = 5 and 9 - 4 = 5 but, 4 - 2 = 2

So, 421 is odd one out.

questions.question_text:
Find the odd one out:
Fingers, Palm, Knee, Wrist
questions.solution: All except Knee are parts of hand.

questions.question_text: MIKE : NJLF :: HAND : ?
questions.solution:
As, MIKE is to NJLF; +1 alphabet

Similarly HAND = IBOE

questions.question_text:
Find the odd one out:
Daman, Pondicherry, Chandigarh, Lucknow
questions.solution: All except Lucknow are Union Territories.

questions.question_text: DELITE : ETILED :: FLOWER : ?
questions.solution: Reverse the letter to get the current form.

questions.question_text: Find the odd one out: Hostel, Hotel, Inn, Club
questions.solution: All except Club are places where people can stay.

questions.question_text: Find the odd one out: Mother, Friend, Sister, Father
questions.solution: All except Friend denote blood relations.

questions.question_text: Choose the word which is different from the rest.
questions.solution: All the things are obtained from crops except chalk.

questions.question_text: Find the odd one out: Kiwi, Eagle, Emu, Ostrich
questions.solution: All except Eagle are flightless birds.

questions.question_text: Identify the odd letter group.
questions.solution:
STU is odd one out.

A + 1 = B + 2 = D

F + 1 = G + 2 = I

L + 1 = M + 2 = O

So, S + 1 = T + 1 = U

questions.question_text: Choose the odd number: 25, 64, 78, 121
questions.solution: Here, each number except 78 is a perfect square.

questions.question_text: Which one is different from the other three alternatives?
questions.solution: Except circle all other are made from straight lines.

questions.question_text:
Which pair does not belong with the others?
13-31, 45-54, 16-61, 71-88
questions.solution: In all other pairs, the second number is obtained by interchanging the digits of the first.

questions.question_text: Select the odd word from the given alternatives.
questions.solution:
64 - 43

1000 - 103

343 - 73

900 - 302

Expect 900, all are perfect cubes.

questions.question_text:
Which pair does not belong with the others?
4 : 16, 6 : 42, 8 : 24, 17 : 54
questions.solution: In all other pairs, second number is a multiple of the first.

questions.question_text: Find the odd one:
questions.solution: All except Igloo are types of boats, while igloo is a kind of house found in the polar system.

questions.question_text:
Find the odd one out:
Thermosphere, Stratosphere, Exosphere, Ionosphere
questions.solution: All except Thermosphere are layers of the atmosphere.

questions.question_text: Find the odd one out: Grapes, Pineapple, Cashew, Apple
questions.solution: All except Cashew are fruits.

questions.question_text:
Find the odd one out:
Valley, Sea, Tower, Mountain
questions.solution: All except Tower are natural geographical features, while the tower is man-made.

questions.question_text: Find the odd one out: Sahara, Thar, Gobi, Sunderban
questions.solution: All except Sunderban are deserts, while Sunderban is forest land.

questions.question_text: Find the odd one out: Tortoise, Snail, Turtle, Spider
questions.solution: All except Spider have hard protective shells.

questions.question_text: Find the odd one out: Galileo, Copernicus, Columbus, Bhaskara
questions.solution: All except Columbus were astronomers, while Columbus was an explorer.

questions.question_text: Find the odd one out: Poland, Greece, Spain, Korea
questions.solution: All except Korea are European countries, while Korea is an Asian country.

questions.question_text: Find the odd one out: Asia, Argentina, Africa, Australia
questions.solution: All except Argentina are continents, while Argentina is a country.

questions.question_text: Find the odd term from the given alternatives.
questions.solution:
All the words in the given options contain only vowels except option ALO.

In ALO, L is a consonant. Hence it is the odd word.

questions.question_text: Which Pair does NOT belong with the others?
questions.solution: In all other pairs, the second number is 9 more than the first.

questions.question_text: Which pair does not belong with the others? 2-8, 3-27, 4-32, 5-125
questions.solution:
In all other pairs, the second number is the cube of the first.

The cube of 4 is 64.

questions.question_text: Which pair does not belong with the others? 80-9, 64-8, 36-6, 7-49
questions.solution:
In all other pairs, one number is the square of the other.

But the square of 9 is 81.

questions.question_text: Which pair does not belong with the others? 22-3, 28-4, 36-5, 43-6
questions.solution: In all other pairs, the first number when divided by the second leaves 1 as the remainder. But 28 is exactly divisible by 4.

questions.question_text:
Select the one which is different from the other three responses.
Acute, Right, Obtuse, Parallel
questions.solution: Except for Parallel, all others are types of angle.

questions.question_text:
Which pair does not belong with the others?
3:4, 4:7, 5:12, 20:21
questions.solution:
In all other pairs, one of the two numbers is prime.

Except 20:21, both are divisible.

questions.question_text:
Select the one which is different from the other three responses.
80, 120, 144, 192
questions.solution: The number 144 is a perfect square among them.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is unfit in the group?
questions.solution:
13 is the only option that has a digits sum equal to 4. The sum of the digits of all the other options is equal to 5. Let us see:

14 = 1 + 4 = 5, 50 = 5 + 0 = 5, 13 = 1 + 3 = 4, 32 = 3 + 2 = 5

questions.question_text: Identify the relationship between each of the given pairs on either side of :: and find the missing term. 6 : 36 :: 4 : ?
questions.solution: The second number is the square of the first number.

questions.question_text: Identify the relationship between each of the given pairs on either side of :: and find the missing term. AG : FL :: EK : ?
questions.solution: Each letter of the first group is moved five steps forward to obtain the corresponding letter of the second group.

questions.question_text:
Choose the odd one out.
Axe, Knife, Hammer, Scissors
questions.solution: Except Hammer, all others are used to cut.

questions.question_text: Clock : Time :: Thermometer : ?
questions.solution: First is an instrument to measure second where the thermometer is used to measure temperature.

questions.question_text:
Identify the relationship between each of the given pairs on either side of :: and find the missing term.
PEARL :  :: SEAL : ?
questions.solution: The second is the correct mirror image of the word SEAL.

questions.question_text: Identify the relationship of the first pair and then find the missing term in the second pair. CDH: HCD :: KPQ : ?
questions.solution: As, Similarly,

questions.question_text: 7 is to 14 as 9 is to ________.
questions.solution: The second number is double the first number on the left of as. So, required term = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 = 18

questions.question_text: 6 is related to 19 in the same way as 4 is related to ________.
questions.solution: The relationship is x is related to 3x + 1.

questions.question_text: 1 is related to 4 in the same way as 25 is related to ________.
questions.solution: The relationship is x is related to 4 {tex}\times{/tex} x.

questions.question_text: 12 is related to 144 in the same way as 17 is related to ________.
questions.solution: The relationship is x is related to x {tex}\times{/tex} x.

questions.question_text: 34 is related to 12 in the same way as 59 is related to ________.
questions.solution: The second number is the product of the digits of the first number.

questions.question_text: 16 is related to 56 as 32 is related to ________.
questions.solution: {tex}\frac{16}{2}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 56, so, {tex}\frac{32}{2}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 112

questions.question_text:
Odd one out.
21, 15, 7, 27
questions.solution: All other numbers are divisible by 3, except 7.
All other numbers are divisible by 10, except 806.
243 is the only odd number.

questions.question_text:

Lion, cat, Rabbit, Fox
questions.solution: Rabbit

questions.question_text: Find the figure which will complete the second pair following the same rule that first pair follow.
questions.solution: The second shape becomes large and merges the first shape in itself after in verting.

questions.question_text:
Identify the relationship between each of the given pairs on either side of :: and find the missing term.
21 : 7 :: 574 : ?
questions.solution:
The relationship is 21 {tex}\div{/tex} 7 = 3

So, 574 {tex}\div{/tex} 7 = 82

questions.question_text:

34 - 43, 62 - 71, 55 - 62, 83 - 92
questions.solution: Except in this option, in all other pairs, second number is 9 more than the first number.

questions.solution: Except in this option, in all other pairs, second number is 10 more than the first number.

CHG, HEG, BBC, LMM

questions.solution: Three letters are not in order as in the reverse order of the English alphabets.

questions.solution: 14 is the only number that is not divisible by 3 or 6.

questions.question_text:

questions.solution: All except February are months with 31 days, while February has 28 or 29 days.

questions.question_text:

Chord, Diagonal, Diameter, Radius
questions.solution: All except diagonal are terms associated with circle.

questions.solution: 37 is the only prime number in the group.

3 - 5, 7 - 3, 13 - 17, 7 - 11

questions.question_text:
Select the odd one out.
AEFJ, KOPT, EHIL, QUVZ

There is a certain relationship between fig. (i) & (ii). Identify the relationship and establish the same relation between figure (iii) & (iv), by selecting the figure from the given options which will replace the (?) in fig.(iv).
questions.solution: The figure is rotating 90o clockwise and inner unshaded elements interchange their places after rotation.
